title: Khmerization: Preah Khan Damaged After Storm Uproots Large Tree [The tree should have been cut down a long time ago. Now the damage to the 1000 year-old temple is irreparable]
highlights: The northern gate of the Preah Khan temple inside the Angkor Archaeological Park was damaged on Friday when a 30-meter-tall tree was uprooted during a heavy rainstorm and came crashing down on the 12th century structure. The tree smashed through eight large stones that supported one of the ancient structure’s main gates, said Bun Narith,  ...    A tree uprooted in a rainstorm on Friday lies on top of the northern gate of Preah Khan temple in Siem Reap province.  ...    “One big tree collapsed on Friday at 11:30 a.m. because of strong wind and heavy rains, which caused damage to Preah  ...    “We have to cut the trunk to move it from the temple, but this is hard to do because of the floodwater from all the raining,” Mr. Narith said. “There is just too much water and it has made the roots of the big trees unstable,” he added.  ...
